description: Scoped filesystem storage — a smart notebook for durable LLM artifacts. Backed by the path in config.json agents.storage.root (default: D:\MCP_Storage). When agents.storage.uncShare is also configured, callers may pass EITHER the local path or the UNC share form — the server translates UNC to local before resolving, so both work transparently. Useful for staging forge inputs and retrieving forge outputs across machines. Forge outputs land at <root>\forge\<tool-name>\* and are also listed in the forge_call response as _outputs (each with an `uncPath` field for direct SMB access when uncShare is configured). For files that are fleeting or temporary and should NOT be included in the VDB semantic index, write them to <root>\temp\*. The VDB agent ignores both the forge/ and temp/ directories (config-level) and respects .nvdb_ignore files in any watched directory.

tools.name: storage_stat
tools.description: Check if a path exists and what type it is (file or directory), without reading content. Returns exists, type, size, and modified time.

tools.name: storage_read
tools.description: Read a file from storage. Returns utf8 text by default; use encoding=base64 for binaries. Files above the inline threshold (default 64 KB) return a URL pointer instead of inline content. The response includes: (1) httpUrl — a fully-qualified URL using the server's LAN IP from config.json agents.storage.publicUrl (currently http://192.168.0.100:3100). Use this URL VERBATIM with fetch_webpage. NEVER change the host to 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1' — those resolve to the client machine, not the server, and the file will be unreachable. (2) nextStep — a copy-pasteable action like 'fetch_webpage({ url: "<httpUrl>" })'. The REST endpoint streams the file directly from disk, bypassing the MCP transport-size limit that kills inline text responses on large files (chat-app client hits 'MCP stream ended without response' around 400 KB). For LAN clients on the same machine, the same file is also reachable via the UNC share configured in agents.storage.uncShare. Spec-compliant alternative: this server also exposes storage files as MCP resources via resources/list and resources/read using the URI scheme storage://<relative-path>.

tools.name: storage_write
tools.description: Write a file to storage. Creates parent directories automatically. Overwrites existing files. Content can be utf8 text or base64-encoded binary. Files above maxWriteSize are rejected.

content.description: File content (string for utf8, base64 string for binary)

tools.name: storage_list
tools.description: List a directory in storage. Returns entries with name, type, size, modified time, and relative path. Use recursive=true for a flat list of all descendants.

recursive.description: List all descendants as a flat list (default: false)

tools.name: storage_move
tools.description: Move or rename a file or directory within storage. Refuses to overwrite an existing destination.

tools.name: storage_delete
tools.description: Delete a file or directory. Requires recursive=true to delete non-empty directories.

path.description: Path to delete within storage root

recursive.description: Required to delete non-empty directories (default: false)

tools.name: storage_search
tools.description: Semantic search over files in storage using the vector database. Returns ranked matches with path, score, and optional content snippet. Filter by folder or file extension.

folder.description: Filter to a specific top-level folder in storage.

extension.description: Filter to files with this extension, e.g. '.md'.

top_k.description: Number of top matches to return.

include_content.description: Include the matched chunk text in results.

tools.name: storage_resources_list
tools.description: MCP Resources bridge: list all storage resources as MCP resource URIs. Returns paginated resources using the custom URI scheme storage://<relative-path>. Each resource has uri, name, mimeType, size, and annotations (directory: true for directories). Use the cursor field to page through large result sets. To read a resource, call storage_resources_read with its uri.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response. Omit for the first page.

tools.name: storage_resources_read
tools.description: MCP Resources bridge: read the content of a storage resource by URI. Returns inline text for small files and a fetchable HTTP URL for files above the inline threshold (default 64 KB). For binary files, set encoding=base64. URI format: storage://<relative-path>. Find URIs with storage_resources_list or storage_resources_templates.

encoding.description: Content encoding (default: utf8 for text, base64 for binaries)

tools.name: storage_resources_templates
tools.description: MCP Resources bridge: list available resource URI templates. Currently exposes storage://{path} for arbitrary files under the storage root.
